title: Double immunofluorescence – Paraffin-embedded sections
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ae0j3","text":"In order to be able to examine the co-distribution of two (or more) different antigens in the same sample, a double","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8nt7m","text":"immunofluorescence procedure can be carried out. Primary antibodies raised in different species can be used either in","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7olp7","text":"parallel (in a mixture) or in a sequential way.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: 1.1. Deparaffinize sections in xylene 2x5 min. 1.2. Hydrate with 100% ethanol 2x3 min. 1.3. Hydrate with 95% ethanol 1 min. 1.4. Rinse in distilled water
2.1 Fix the samples either in ice-cold methanol, acetone (1-10 min) or in 3-4% paraformaldehyde in PBS pH 7.4 for 15 min at room temperature. 2.2 Wash the samples twice with ice cold PBS.
3.1 Incubate the samples for 10 min with PBS containing 0.25% Triton X-100 3.2 Wash cells in PBS three times for 5 mins.

4.1. Incubate tissues with 1% BSA in PBST for 30 min to block unspecific binding of the antibodies 4.2. Incubate tissues in the mixture of two primary antibodies in 1% BSA in PBST in a humidified chamber for overnight at 4 o C. 4.3. Decant the mixture solution and wash the cells three times in PBS, 5 mins each wash. 4.4. Incubate cells with the mixture of two secondary antibodies which are raised in different species in 1% BSA for 1 hr at room temperature in dark. 4.5. Decant the mixture of the secondary antibody solution and wash three times with PBS for 5 min each in dark.
5.1. Rinse with PBS. 5.2 Incubate tissues on 0.1-1 µg/ml DAPI for 1 min.
6.1. Seal coverslip with nail polish to prevent drying and movement under microscope. 6.2. Store in dark at -20°C or 4°C.
creator: Raheleh Roudi
